title: Debugging & Problem Solving
description: Learn systematic approaches to find and fix errors in your code.
fullDescription: Develop problem-solving skills by learning how to identify and fix errors in code. Understand common mistakes and how to debug programs effectively.

teacherInstructions.setup: Prepare three pre-bugged example programs and a visible debugging checklist poster.
steps: Normalize mistakes by sharing a bug from a previous lesson (3 min).
Teach a four-step debugging routine: reproduce, inspect, test, fix (6 min).
Students debug guided examples in pairs (9 min).
Class debrief on strategies that found bugs fastest (5 min).
discussionPrompts: What clues told you where the bug was?
Why is changing one thing at a time important while debugging?
What debugging habit will you reuse in future lessons?
tips: Require students to predict the bug before editing code.
Promote pair-debugging language: driver and reviewer roles.
Track common bug patterns and revisit them weekly.

teacherGuide: Normalize mistakes and emphasize that debugging is a normal part of programming. Teach systematic debugging techniques like adding print statements and testing small sections. Have students practice fixing buggy programs.

studentExercises.title: Find the Bug
studentExercises.description: Identify and fix a simple error in provided code

studentExercises.title: Debug a Program
studentExercises.description: Debug a more complex program with multiple issues

studentExercises.title: Problem Solving
studentExercises.description: Fix a program and improve its efficiency
